summary: The Docker build failed when a RUN step attempted to wget a nydus release asset and received an HTTP 502, causing the build to exit with code 1 and abort the bake.
root_cause: A wget command in the Dockerfile failed with HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway when downloading the nydus release asset, causing the Docker build layer to exit with code 1.
relevant_tests: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the network request to GitHub returned a 502 error during the wget.

evidence.quote: ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Shows the failing Docker RUN command and that it caused the build to exit with code 1.
Identifies the Dockerfile line where the wget was executed.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Shows the overall buildx bake step failed due to the wget failure.

suggested_fix: Retry the CI job (the failure appears to be a transient HTTP 502). To make the build more robust, modify the Dockerfile to download with retries/timeouts (e.g. add wget flags like --tries and --waitretry or use curl with retries), or vendor/cache the nydus release artifact in CI or a known accessible location so the build does not depend on a single external fetch.

summary: A functional test failed because a pip install command returned exit code 1 after pip raised an OSError due to repeated HTTP 502 responses when trying to fetch a GitHub-hosted package, causing the test assertion to fail.
root_cause: pip failed to download https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/... due to repeated HTTP 502 responses (OSError), causing the install command to exit with code 1 and triggering the test assertion failure.

evidence.reason: Shows the specific test that failed and that the failure was due to a script returning a non-zero exit code.
Identifies the exact pip install command that was executed by the test.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Shows pip raised an OSError due to repeated HTTP 502 responses from GitHub while attempting the download.
Confirms the pip command exited with a non-zero return code, which the test framework treated as a failure.
Indicates the CI job failed because the test-suite exit status was non-zero.

suggested_fix: Rerun the CI job to confirm whether this was a transient GitHub/network outage. If failures recur, investigate runner network egress and proxy/load-balancer logs or switch the test to use a cached/local copy of the test package or add retries/backoff around the pip download.

summary: A Jest test in test/config/get.ts failed because it expected the registry URL without a trailing slash but received the same URL with a trailing slash, causing the test suite to exit with status 1.
root_cause: The test asserted exact string equality for the registry URL without a trailing slash, but the code/environment returned the URL with a trailing slash, causing the assertion to fail.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Shows the specific test file that failed under Jest.

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Shows the exact string the test expected (no trailing slash).
Shows the actual value returned by the code (with trailing slash), which differs by a trailing slash.
Demonstrates the test uses an exact equality assertion that does not tolerate a trailing slash.
Shows the test runner command and that the failure originated from running Jest.
Indicates the failing test caused the process to exit non-zero, failing the CI job.

suggested_fix: Either normalize the registry URL to remove a trailing slash before returning it, or relax the test assertion to tolerate a trailing slash (for example, compare after trimming a trailing '/' or use a matcher that ignores a terminal slash). Update test/config/get.ts to assert e.g. expect(stdout.toString().trim().replace(/\/$/, '')).toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com') or adjust the code to strip trailing slashes from registry values.
